stocks.recent_news: Stock at ~Rs 686.75 (+2.02%) on Apr 23, 2026, sitting near 52-week high (Rs 685); market cap ~Rs 17,113 cr. Board meeting confirmed for Apr 29, 2026 to approve Q4/FY26 audited results and consider final dividend. Q3 FY26 was strong: revenue Rs 1,387.9 cr (+22% YoY), PAT Rs 150.21 cr (+27.7% YoY), EBITDA +34% YoY with margin expanding 196bp to 22.2%, driven by Finished Dosages in North America/Europe.
stocks.sector_context: Indian pharma sector outlook constructive - Paracetamol API segment projected ~6-8% CAGR through 2026 supported by formulation exports and rising domestic capacity utilization. Granules holds ~12% global Paracetamol market share. Recently commissioned new 10,000-ton Hyderabad facility. USFDA compliance remains the key sector swing factor for US-facing formulators.
stocks.peer_comparison: Outperforming many mid-cap pharma peers on Q3 margin expansion and revenue growth (+22% YoY vs sector single-digit). Stock trading at top of analyst consensus target band (Rs 587-687), implying limited consensus upside without an earnings beat. Pivot to complex drugs has hit FDA roadblocks unlike select peers.
red_flags: Feb 2025 USFDA warning letter at Gagillapur facility still in remediation; new drug application approvals only expected Q1 FY26
Stock at/above analyst consensus target band heading into Q4 print - binary event risk
Limited valuation cushion at 52-week high if FY27 guidance disappoints
Complex-drug pivot has faced FDA roadblocks per recent reports
catalysts: Q4 FY26 audited results + FY27 guidance on Apr 29, 2026
Final dividend recommendation at Apr 29 board meeting
Resolution updates on Gagillapur USFDA remediation

stocks.recent_news: Stock rallied ~6% over Apr 21-22, 2026 (+4% on Apr 21 alone), bouncing ~18% from 52-week low of Rs 2,023.05 hit on Apr 2, 2026; trading in Rs 2,150-2,400 range. Q4 FY26 board meeting and earnings conference call scheduled for Apr 30, 2026 with final dividend on agenda. Volume-over-margin strategy currently under analyst scrutiny; mixed core earnings signals.
stocks.sector_context: Nifty FMCG index -6% YTD 2026 on rural growth concerns and FII rotation, but late-April rally on improving rural demand narrative. Rural India now ~51% of FMCG volumes with premium/small-pack growth outpacing urban. Dual threat from poor monsoon outlook and returning commodity inflation flagged for FY27. ICICI Securities views HUL as structurally better positioned to navigate inflationary cycle via pricing power.
stocks.peer_comparison: Largest Indian FMCG by market cap (~Rs 5.5 trillion). P/E ~36-49x sits between ITC (~18x) and Nestle (~76x). Rural revenue mix ~35-40% - less rural-exposed than Dabur (45-50%). Analyst preference has tilted to Nestle in the FMCG pack; HUL consensus 12M targets Rs 2,607-2,719.
red_flags: Sector valuation pressure as rural growth slows and margin pressure mounts
Poor monsoon outlook and commodity inflation risk for FY27
Stock has already rallied ~18% off Apr 2 low - much of recovery thesis may be priced in ahead of Apr 30 print
Premium P/E (~36-49x) leaves limited margin of safety on slower volume growth
catalysts: Q4 FY26 results and earnings call on Apr 30, 2026
FY27 guidance commentary on rural recovery and pricing power
Continued positive read-through from FMCG peer prints
